novel_features.name: Incident-tag dry-run
novel_features.description: Tag every conversation mentioning a phrase in a time window with one safe-by-default command.
novel_features.rationale: Calls /conversations/search server-side, then fans out tag mutations through the adaptive limiter; default is dry-run with a diff, --apply mutates.
novel_features.why_it_matters: When an incident hits, tagging 50 conversations by hand is the bottleneck; this is the single command that closes the loop without spreadsheets.

novel_features.name: Articles git round-trip
novel_features.description: Flatten every help-center article into a markdown tree you can edit in git, then push changes back.
novel_features.rationale: Paginates /articles + /help_center/collections, writes <id>-<slug>.<locale>.md with YAML frontmatter; push diffs against the last-pull manifest and PATCHes only what changed.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Lets docs teams version-control help-center content alongside product code; multilingual articles land as sibling files.

novel_features.name: Contact 360
novel_features.description: One nested payload joining a contact across companies, conversations, tickets, notes, and tags.
novel_features.rationale: Resolves the contact, then SQL-joins synced companies (via attached_companies), conversations (where contact is author), tickets, notes, and tags from the local store.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Replaces 4-6 separate API calls and a mental merge; this is the lookup an agent makes before every triage decision.

novel_features.name: SLA analytics
novel_features.description: First-response and resolution-time metrics grouped by team or admin, computed over the local store.
novel_features.rationale: SQL over conversations join conversation_parts (min admin-author timestamp minus conversation created_at) join teams; emits JSON/CSV.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Replaces the weekly export-to-spreadsheet ritual; the API alone can't compute this in one call.

narrative.headline: Every Intercom resource as a typed CLI with offline sync and EU/AU regions.
narrative.value_prop: This CLI exposes the full Intercom REST API as a single Go binary. Every endpoint is a subcommand. A `sync` command mirrors conversations, contacts, companies, tickets, and articles into a local SQLite database so offline `search` and four headline transcendence commands — `incident-tag`, `articles pull/push`, `contact 360`, and `conversations sla` — answer the questions the API alone can't. Region-aware (`--region us|eu|au`) and built for agents (JSON-first, typed exit codes, MCP-mirrored).
narrative.auth_narrative: Intercom uses Bearer access tokens (PAT-style). Export `INTERCOM_ACCESS_TOKEN` or run `intercom-pp-cli auth set-token "<token>"` to save it to your config. The CLI pins the `Intercom-Version: 2.13` header on every request. Tokens are workspace-scoped; pick the right region with `--region us|eu|au` or `INTERCOM_REGION`.

quickstart.comment: Save your access token; or just export INTERCOM_ACCESS_TOKEN; verify with `doctor`.
Confirm auth + reachability before anything destructive.
Mirror the four big resources into the local store so offline queries work.
One nested payload joining a contact's companies, conversations, tickets, notes, and tags.
SLA metrics computed locally from synced conversations + parts.

troubleshoots.fix: Your token might be wrong region. Re-set it with `intercom-pp-cli auth set-token <token>` and add `--region eu` (or `au`) on every command, or `export INTERCOM_REGION=eu`.
Sync retries automatically with adaptive backoff respecting `X-RateLimit-Reset`. Re-run after a few seconds, or pass `--max-pages` to cap fan-out.
Run `sync --resources conversations,conversation_parts` first — SLA metrics need parts to compute first-response time.
Articles changed in Intercom since your last `pull`. Re-pull to refresh the manifest, then re-edit.

narrative.when_to_use: Use intercom-pp-cli when you need to run the same Intercom operation across many records, when the official MCP doesn't reach your workspace (EU/AU), when you need offline analytics the API doesn't compute (first-response SLA, contact 360 joins), or when you're building an agent that triages, tags, or summarizes conversations and needs every Intercom action as a callable tool.

recipes.title: Tag every conversation mentioning an outage
recipes.explanation: Search server-side, dry-run by default, --apply fans out tag mutations through the adaptive limiter.

recipes.title: Find slow-response teams this week
recipes.explanation: Local SQL over conversations + parts; --select narrows the nested payload so agents don't burn context.

recipes.title: Pull every help-center article into a git-managed tree
recipes.explanation: Each article lands as `<id>-<slug>.<locale>.md` with YAML frontmatter; commit the directory to git, edit locally, then run `intercom-pp-cli articles push --from ./articles/` to sync diffs back.

recipes.title: Resolve a contact before triaging
recipes.explanation: Joins five entities locally; replaces 4-6 separate API calls and removes the agent's need to template Intercom's nested search predicate.

recipes.title: List conversations updated this week with --select to keep payload small
recipes.explanation: Generated endpoint mirror; `--select` uses dotted paths to walk Intercom's nested envelope so agents don't load 30 KB per conversation.
